original text:
I'm trying to setup my TP-LINK TL-WDN3200 adapter.
lsusb can see the device.
I do not know what to do from here.
But what could be wrong?
Warning: Symbol version dump /usr/src/linux-3.5.7-gentoo/Modules.symvers is missing; modules will have no dependencies and modversions.
The kernel had the CONFIG_MODVERSIONS option enabled.
I've disabled it and rebuild my kernel.
I've compiled and installed the drivers.
My system tells me that I am connected to the network.
I can see it even on my router, but still I have no internet access.
I can't ping internal or external IPs.
Any suggestion?
I've tried to disable security on my router and it was working without it but I'd like to use security.
I have a wpa2 personal with AES encryption wireless network.
config_ra0="192.168.1.11 netmask 255.255.255.0 brd 192.168.1.255"
WPA: Installing GTK to the driver (keyidx=1 tx=0 len=16).
Is anybody can help to get the security working?
processed_text:
I'm trying to setup my TP-LINK TL-WDN3200 adapter.
lsusb can see the device.
I do not know what to do from here.
But what could be wrong?
Warning: Symbol version dump /usr/src/linux-3.5.7-gentoo/Modules.symvers is missing; modules will have no dependencies and modversions.
The kernel had the CONFIG_MODVERSIONS option enabled.
I've disabled it and rebuild my kernel.
I've compiled and installed the drivers.
My system tells me that I am connected to the network.
I can see it even on my router, but still I have no internet access.
I can't ping internal or external IPs.
Any suggestion?
I've tried to disable security on my router and it was working without it but I'd like to use security.
I have a wpa2 personal with AES encryption wireless network.
config_ra0=" netmask  brd "
WPA: Installing GTK to the driver (keyidx=1 tx=0 len=16).
